demographics.living_situation: at home, alone at the time of contact

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I suddenly felt a sharp pain in my neck an hour ago, and now there is a large lump there and it constantly feels like I have a pill stuck in my throat.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on out of nowhere about an hour ago — the pain was sharp and then this lump appeared really fast, and now it feels like something is stuck in my throat. I'm scared it's going to block my breathing or that it's something serious.

symptom_narrative.onset: about an hour ago, completely out of the blue — I was fine before that
symptom_narrative.duration: roughly an hour, the lump is still there
symptom_narrative.character: a sharp pain at the front-right of my neck that came on suddenly, and a lump that swelled up quickly in the same spot
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the pain was quite sharp when it started; now it's more of a dull ache with an annoying stuck feeling
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the front of my neck, more on the right side
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing really — I keep swallowing to try to clear the stuck feeling but it doesn't help
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: swallowing hard makes the sharp pain worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: it feels like there's a pill stuck in my throat and swallowing is a bit uncomfortable; no fever, my voice sounds normal to me, and I can breathe okay
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: never had anything like this before; I've never noticed a lump in my neck until today
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can do everything normally but I'm very anxious and keep touching my neck and swallowing

family_history_as_reported.narrative: No one in my family has had thyroid cancer or neck problems that I know of.

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I have never had radiation treatment to my neck.

communication_style.typical_message_style: worried, asks whether the lump could suffocate her or be cancer

objective_data_offered.value: a lump I can see and feel at the front-right of my neck, maybe a couple of centimetres across, that came up suddenly this hour
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-observed and self-measured; size is the patient's own estimate, not a measured or examined finding

objective_data_offered.value: when I swallow, the lump seems to move up and down a little in my neck
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient self-observation; not a clinician-confirmed examination finding

objective_data_offered.value: I can breathe fine, no wheezing or noisy breathing, and my voice sounds normal to me
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: reassuring self-report only; the patient cannot examine her own airway and this does not exclude progression
